3. REQUIRED TOOLS: read, grep, glob.
4. MUST DO: Focus on local .doom.d and Doom's editor/multiple-cursors and default bindings files. Prefer the actual configured key paths over generic upstream docs.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not inspect unrelated Emacs modules.
6. CONTEXT: User already understands evil-multiedit and now wants to learn evil-mc specifically in this Doom setup.

<content>1: #+title:    :editor multiple-cursors
2: #+subtitle: Make all your mistakes at once
3: #+created:  October 13, 2021
4: #+since:    21.12.0
5: 
6: * Description :unfold:
7: This module adds a multiple cursors implementation to Emacs (two, if you use
8: evil) that loosely take after multi-cursors in Atom or Sublime Text.
9: 
10: ** Maintainers
11: - [[doom-user:][@hlissner]]
12: 
13: [[doom-contrib-maintainer:][Become a maintainer?]]
14: 
15: ** Module flags
16: /This module has no flags./
17: 
18: ** Packages
19: - if [[doom-module::editor evil]]
20:   - [[doom-package:evil-multiedit]]
21:   - [[doom-package:evil-mc]]
22: - else
23:   - [[doom-package:multiple-cursors]]
24: 
25: ** Hacks
26: - Attempts to smooth over [[doom-package:multiple-cursors]]' incompatibilities with [[doom-package:evil]], which
27:   is used internally in some third party plugins.
28: - Attempts to smooth over [[doom-package:evil-mc]]'s incompatibilities with other packages, like
29:   [[doom-package:lispy]].
30: 
31: ** TODO Changelog
32: # This section will be machine generated. Don't edit it by hand.
33: /This module does not have a changelog yet./
34: 
35: * Installation
36: [[id:01cffea4-3329-45e2-a892-95a384ab2338][Enable this module in your ~doom!~ block.]]
37: 
38: /This module has no external requirements./
39: 
40: * TODO Usage
41: #+begin_quote
42:  󱌣 /This module's usage documentation is incomplete./ [[doom-contrib-module:][Complete it?]]
43: #+end_quote
44: 
45: ** evil-mc
46: - The [[doom-package:evil-mc]] keys are under the [[kbd:][gz]] prefix, e.g.
47:   - [[kbd:][gzz]] to toggle new (frozen) cursors at point.
48:   - [[kbd:][gzt]] to toggle mirroring on and off (or switch to insert mode to activate
49:     them).
50:   - [[kbd:][gzA]] to place cursors at the end of each selected line.
51:   - [[kbd:][gzI]] will place them at the beginning.
52: - There's also the ex command ~:mc/REGEXP/FLAGS~, for laying down cursors by
53:   regex.
54: 
55: ** evil-multiedit
56: Designates "interactive edit" regions. Only the textual changes to them are mirrored.
57: 
58: - [[kbd:][M-d]] to iedit the symbol at point. Again to iedit its next match.
59: - [[kbd:][M-S-d]] to do it backwards.
60: - [[kbd:][R]] (in visual mode) to iedit all matches of the selection at point in the
61:   buffer.
62: - Or ~:iedit/REGEX~ to iedit all matches of REGEX.
63: 
64: * TODO Configuration
65: #+begin_quote
66:  󱌣 This module has no configuration documentation yet. [[doom-contrib-module:][Write some?]]
67: #+end_quote
68: 
69: * Troubleshooting
70: /There are no known problems with this module./ [[doom-report:][Report one?]]
71: 
72: * Frequently asked questions
73: /This module has no FAQs yet./ [[doom-suggest-faq:][Ask one?]]
74: 
75: * Appendix

78: #+end_quote
